title: Abnormal liver enzyme review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Requires review of previous results, past medical history, current condition, the specific abnormal analyte, and clinical context rather than inferring significance from magnitude alone.
assessment.rationale: The exact BSG section requires review of previous results, current condition, the specific abnormal analyte, and clinical context rather than inferring significance from magnitude alone.

source.title: Guidelines on the management of abnormal liver blood tests
source.population: Children and adults with abnormal liver blood tests, with recommendation-specific adult and paediatric differences.
source.setting: Primary and secondary care assessment of abnormal liver blood tests.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected result-context, history, adult liver-aetiology-screen, and clinician-referral documentation. The BSG resource page was last reviewed in 2021 with review due in 2025; UAE pathways and patient-specific interpretation require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 2–3 — prior results and clinical context

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires review of previous results, past medical history, current condition, the specific abnormal analyte, and clinical context rather than inferring significance from magnitude alone.

assessment.text: Supports age, hepatitis-risk context, jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, prescribed or non-prescribed drug history, travel or exposure context, and alcohol history.
assessment.rationale: The exact clinical-history section supports clinician-entered jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, medicine, travel, exposure, and alcohol context plus assessed examination and concern.

exact_location.heading: Response to abnormal liver blood tests — clinical history

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports age, hepatitis-risk context, jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, prescribed or non-prescribed drug history, travel or exposure context, and alcohol history.

assessment.text: Supports clinician review of bilirubin and standard liver blood-test components; it does not provide patient-specific interpretation or diagnosis.
assessment.rationale: The exact standard-panel section supports recording reviewed bilirubin and liver blood-test components without interpreting a patient result.

assessment.text: Supports clinician-led further investigation or referral based on synthetic failure, suspicious symptoms or signs, analyte pattern, persistence, risk factors, and clinical context without automatically assigning urgency.
assessment.rationale: The exact response pathway supports clinician-entered investigation, referral, safety-net, and follow-up status without generating an action or urgency.
